mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward 20-day returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) across rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.5%, labor market slack suppresses corporate wage costs and consumer spending growth, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in > 55% of months where UNRATE exceeds 5.5%, or the signal out-of-sample hit rate drops below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts signals broad-based demand recovery, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive growth condition yields a negative mean forward 60-day return, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases, a monthly decline in the index of industrial production indicates lingering supply-side bottlenecks that delay earnings revisions, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: In post-shock recovery windows, negative INDPRO changes correlate with positive 5-day forward returns, or the regime-specific alpha decays to zero within 12 months.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained low inflation readings below 2.0% reduce real interest rate pressures and extend economic expansion expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL levels below 2.0% fail to predict positive 60-day returns, with a rolling Rank IC consistently below 0.01 over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: When real personal consumption expenditures growth falls below 0.3% month-over-month, consumer demand weakness triggers earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in > 60% of instances where RSAFS MoM growth is below 0.3%, or the threshold signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, boosting corporate revenue expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint labor tightening signal produces negative mean forward 20-day returns, or the interaction effect loses statistical significance when controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansion regimes, accelerating industrial production growth signals capacity utilization approaching maximums, prompting inflation fears and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: During late-cycle expansions, positive INDPRO growth correlates with positive 5-day forward returns, or the regime-conditional predictive accuracy falls below 40%.
